task: Launch-quality advanced task: HBM Package Thermal Path Bottleneck Attribution. Reality context: an AI accelerator package throttles under memory-bandwidth stress. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=power_W, metric2=theta_jc_C_W, metric3=substrate_temp_C, metric4=throttle_margin_C; records: R1 power_W=17.83 theta_jc_C_W=43.8 substrate_temp_C=87.3 throttle_margin_C=24.2; R2 power_W=22.12 theta_jc_C_W=31.9 substrate_temp_C=32.3 throttle_margin_C=67; R3 power_W=62.11 theta_jc_C_W=42.5 substrate_temp_C=68.8 throttle_margin_C=32.6; R4 power_W=11.04 theta_jc_C_W=43.7 substrate_temp_C=38.2 throttle_margin_C=64.4; limit_power_W=76.43; limit_theta_jc_C_W=39.3; limit_substrate_temp_C=36; decision_budget_usd=295000; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="career posting emphasized judgment under ambiguous production evidence". Rules and constraints: Use worst-case scoring: normalize each metric to the embedded maximum, apply weights 0.40, 0.30, 0.20, 0.10, and classify the top quartile as immediate action. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: isolate package-level thermal bottleneck; calculate derived metrics, classify every record, identify the smallest defensible intervention, and write the decision rationale. Deliver hbm_thermal_path.xlsx + package_action.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
